Chunk ID: 767011 Wall time: 0.3835 seconds Process exited with code 0 Original token count: 1011 Output: {"comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOS97bAM8AAAABINaetg","author":{"login":"linear-code"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"
ADA-43 Condense repository collaboration HTTP and persistence glue

Refactor the collaboration delivery surface so invite/member behavior is easier to maintain without changing the domain rules or API behavior. Audit evidence: * `api/src/http/repos.rs` now spans 573 lines and owns unrelated repo workflows plus collaboration list/create/update/delete, invite lookup/accept, repeated `require_scope_user`/`find_repo`/owner checks, metadata mutation calls, re-reading the repo to publish change events, and response hydration. * `api/src/http/responses.rs` spans 818 lines and mixes collaboration request/response DTOs with account/session/repo/review/history DTOs. It already has a `responses/projections.rs` precedent for splitting response ownership by behavior. * `api/src/db/repo_collaboration.rs` correctly calls domain behavior, but `mutate_repository_member` and `mutate_repository_invite` duplicate the same Postgres/Memory load-mutatesave transaction wrapper with only the return type changing. * Existing broader Linear work covers role semantics (`ADA-30`, `ADA-31`) and persistence normalization (`ADA-36`), so this issue should stay scoped to code organization and duplicated orchestration. Implementation direction: * Move collaboration route handlers from `api/src/http/repos.rs` into a behavior-owned module such as `api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs`, leaving route wiring thin. * Move collaboration DTOs and response builders out of `api/src/http/responses.rs` into a collaboration-owned response module while preserving TypeScript export generation. * Add a small helper for the repeated owner-read/mutate/publish/hydrate flow used by create/update/delete invite/member endpoints. * If it stays simple, replace `mutate_repository_member` and `mutate_repository_invite` with one generic collaboration mutation helper that preserves the current Memory and Postgres behavior. Acceptance criteria: * No public API shape change for collaboration endpoints. * Invite creation/refresh/revocation/acceptance, member permission update, member removal, and repo change event behavior remain unchanged. * Existing repo lifecycle/collaboration tests pass. * `cargo fmt -- --check`, `cargo test`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings` pass in `api`. * `pnpm check` passes in `web` so generated API contracts stay aligned. Non-goals: * No role/state-machine redesign. * No repository table normalization. * No change to invite token format, TTL, permissions, or event semantics.

ADA-43 Condense repository collaboration HTTP and persistence glue

Refactor the collaboration delivery surface so invite/member behavior is easier to maintain without changing the domain rules or API behavior. Audit evidence: * `api/src/http/repos.rs` now spans 573 lines and owns unrelated repo workflows plus collaboration list/create/update/delete, invite lookup/accept, repeated `require_scope_user`/`find_repo`/owner checks, metadata mutation calls, re-reading the repo to publish change events, and response hydration. * `api/src/http/responses.rs` spans 818 lines and mixes collaboration request/response DTOs with account/session/repo/review/history DTOs. It already has a `responses/projections.rs` precedent for splitting response ownership by behavior. * `api/src/db/repo_collaboration.rs` correctly calls domain behavior, but `mutate_repository_member` and `mutate_repository_invite` duplicate the same Postgres/Memory load-mutatesave transaction wrapper with only the return type changing. * Existing broader Linear work covers role semantics (`ADA-30`, `ADA-31`) and persistence normalization (`ADA-36`), so this issue should stay scoped to code organization and duplicated orchestration. Implementation direction: * Move collaboration route handlers from `api/src/http/repos.rs` into a behavior-owned module such as `api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs`, leaving route wiring thin. * Move collaboration DTOs and response builders out of `api/src/http/responses.rs` into a collaboration-owned response module while preserving TypeScript export generation. * Add a small helper for the repeated owner-read/mutate/publish/hydrate flow used by create/update/delete invite/member endpoints. * If it stays simple, replace `mutate_repository_member` and `mutate_repository_invite` with one generic collaboration mutation helper that preserves the current Memory and Postgres behavior. Acceptance criteria: * No public API shape change for collaboration endpoints. * Invite creation/refresh/revocation/acceptance, member permission update, member removal, and repo change event behavior remain unchanged. * Existing repo lifecycle/collaboration tests pass. * `cargo fmt -- --check`, `cargo test`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings` pass in `api`. * `pnpm check` passes in `web` so generated API contracts stay aligned. Non-goals: * No role/state-machine redesign. * No repository table normalization. * No change to invite token format, TTL, permissions, or event semantics.

Review in Linear
